replication_study.hypothesis: In first-stage effort decisions, participants in the two-stage tournament (TS; treat=2) exert higher effort (effort_t1) than participants in the one-stage tournament (OS; treat=1).

data_plan.source_type: Laboratory experiment decision and questionnaire data (z-Tree outputs cleaned into CSV).
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Main treatments: OS (treat=1) vs TS (treat=2); first-stage effort only (effort_t1).
data_plan.sample_size: 312 subjects with non-missing treat and effort_t1.
data_plan.access_details: Provided in the replication package under /app/data (to be mounted).

qualification.explanation: The dataset reproduces the original laboratory environment (OS vs TS treatments, identical effort and reward structure) but represents an independent sample suitable for testing robustness of the focal claim.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Same core design and measures as the original: OS vs TS treatments, outcome is first-stage effort, and the focal comparison is a nonparametric ranksum/Mann-Whitney test. Evidence: DataDictionary.csv documents treat coding (1=OS, 2=TS) and core variables (effort_t1). The original paper details this comparison and its equilibrium context (original_paper.pdf summary). The post_registration.json confirms the experimental nature (BonnEconLab, OS and TS treatments, effort choice in discrete units).
qualification.deviation_from_original: The replication dataset appears larger for the OS/TS comparison than the original main OS/TS sample. Original main OS/TS sample size reported as 124 (post_registration.json), while the replication dataset has 312 entries across treat=1 and treat=2 with non-missing effort_t1 (FinalData.csv inspection). Also, the replication dataset here focuses on OS and TS only (DataDictionary.csv shows treat only 1 and 2), whereas the original study also includes THS and OSL (post_registration.json, original_paper.pdf), indicating this replication targets the main treatments without the auxiliary ones.

data_plan.notes: Variables present include: treat (1=OS, 2=TS), effort_t1, effort_t2, random/output/cost variables, demographics. DataDictionary.csv documents variable meanings; FinalData.csv contains 24 columns with complete coverage for treat and effort_t1. The Stata .do file references raw z-Tree cleaning and a ranksum test on first-stage effort; we translated the focal test to Python and operate directly on the cleaned FinalData.csv. All code IO paths use /app/data per execution requirements.

planned_method.steps: 1) Load /app/data/FinalData.csv. 2) Subset to observations with treat in {1,2} and non-missing effort_t1. 3) Compute descriptive statistics of effort_t1 by treat (1=OS, 2=TS). 4) Conduct a two-sided Mann-Whitney U test comparing effort_t1 between OS and TS, mirroring Stata ranksum. 5) Save descriptive summary and test results to /app/data. 6) Compare direction and significance to the original claim (TS > OS; p≈0.011 reported).
planned_method.models: Nonparametric comparison of central tendency: Mann-Whitney U (equivalent to Stata ranksum), two-sided.
planned_method.tools_software: Python 3.10; pandas for data I/O and summaries; scipy (mannwhitneyu) for the test; numpy for numerical support.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Difference in central tendency of effort_t1 between TS and OS (report group means/medians and rank-biserial effect size).
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Mann-Whitney U test (two-sided) comparing TS vs OS on effort_t1.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion for missing treat or effort_t1.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Two-sided alpha=0.05; confirm direction TS > OS. Reject null if p<0.05.

files.replication_data/data cleaning and analy: Python translation of the focal analysis from the Stata .do file. Reads /app/data/FinalData.csv, filters to treat ∈ {1,2}, computes descriptive stats for effort_t1 by treatment, and runs a two-sided Mann-Whitney U test (OS vs TS). Outputs: /app/data/effort_t1_by_treatment_summary.csv and /app/data/mannwhitney_effort_t1_OS_vs_TS.json.

codebase.notes: All IO is constrained to /app/data. The original Stata script was not executed due to absolute paths and OS-specific commands; its focal ranksum was translated to Python. Dependencies limited to pandas, numpy, and scipy. The script assumes FinalData.csv is present under /app/data with columns treat and effort_t1 per DataDictionary.csv.

analysis.instructions: 1) Ensure FinalData.csv and DataDictionary.csv are available under the mounted path ./data mapped to /app/data. 2) Run: python replication_data/data cleaning and analysis__py.py. 3) Inspect outputs at /app/data/effort_t1_by_treatment_summary.csv and /app/data/mannwhitney_effort_t1_OS_vs_TS.json. 4) Compare the direction and p-value to the original (expected TS > OS; original Mann-Whitney z=2.536, p=0.011 two-sided per initial_details.txt and original_paper.pdf).
analysis.comparison_metrics: Primary: Mann-Whitney U test p-value (two-sided) and effect direction (TS > OS). Secondary: group means/medians of effort_t1 and rank-biserial correlation. Compare replication p-value and direction to original report (z=2.536, p=0.011).
